title: Pleurophragma lilacinum
intro: Pleurophragma lilacinum, also known as Red Pleurophragma, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pleurophragma lilacinum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pleurophragma lilacinum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Pleurophragma lilacinum is a species of freshwater algae in the family Cymbellaceae. It belongs to the phylum Ochrophyta and class Bacillariophyceae. The kingdom to which it belongs is Protista. The class Bacillariophyceae is part of the division Bacillariophyta. This classification places Pleurophragma lilacinum within the broader taxonomic group of diatoms.
